Memory safety requirements: all buffers containing plaintext credentials or derived keys MUST be allocated in non-swappable memory (mlock), MUST be zeroized with a volatile write pattern to prevent compiler optimization, and MUST have a maximum lifetime of 5 seconds enforced by a watchdog timer that force-zeroizes on timeout
Legacy Bridge.api_contract: Core interfaces: CredentialVault manages the encrypted vault file with methods store(service, credential_type, plaintext) -> credential_id, retrieve(service, credential_id) -> plaintext (TPM-gated), delete(service, credential_id) -> void, list(service?) -> metadata[], and health_check() -> health_report[]. CredentialInjector provides inject(credential_id, request_context) -> augmented_request, handling decryption, injection, and zeroization in a single atomic operation. SyncManager exposes initiate_sync(peer_device_key, credential_ids[]) -> sync_result and receive_sync(sync_payload) -> sync_result, managing the DH exchange, encryption, transmission over WireGuard, and re-encryption lifecycle. DashboardIndicator provides get_status(service) -> {color: green|yellow|red, reason?: string} and get_all_statuses() -> status_map. OAuthRefresher provides refresh_if_needed(credential_id) -> refresh_result for automatic token lifecycle management. Internal events emitted: credential_stored, credential_accessed, credential_deleted, credential_synced, credential_flagged, oauth_refreshed, sync_failed. All methods that access plaintext credentials require an active TPM session and return TPM-unavailable errors if the hardware is not accessible.
dependencies: TPM 2.0 or Secure Enclave hardware and platform API for key storage and HKDF operations
Device Key (Tier 2) from the Loc'd Key Hierarchy (Section 4.2) as the root of credential encryption
WireGuard mesh connectivity (Section 9) for cross-device credential synchronization
Delegation Layer (Section 6) to verify that the device is authorized before allowing credential operations
Identity Layer (Section 5) for determining whether a service supports native Loc'd auth (green indicator) via _locd-svc DNS lookup
X25519 Diffie-Hellman implementation (RFC 7748) for sync key exchange
CBOR encoding/decoding library (RFC 8949) for vault file and sync message serialization
BLAKE2b hashing for credential reuse detection
Platform-specific memory locking API (mlock/VirtualLock) for non-swappable credential buffers
OAuth 2.0 client library for automatic token refresh flows (RFC 6749)

Recovery System.summary: The Recovery System module implements identity recovery mechanisms for the Loc'd protocol, providing users with secure pathways to restore their Master Key when their primary device is lost, stolen, or destroyed. It encompasses USB security key backup using FIDO2 resident credentials with Argon2id + XChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption, support for multiple geographically distributed recovery keys, optional Shamir's Secret Sharing for social recovery with configurable thresholds, and graceful handling of the irrecoverable identity case where no backup exists. The module enforces the protocol's core principle that identity is user-sovereign with no backdoors, meaning recovery without a valid backup results in permanent identity loss by design.
features: USB Recovery Key creation: export Master Key from secure enclave in encrypted form, encrypt with user passphrase via Argon2id (64MB memory, 3 iterations, 4 parallelism) + XChaCha20-Poly1305, and write to USB key's FIDO2 resident credential storage

USB Recovery Key restoration: install client on new device, authenticate with USB key (physical presence button press + passphrase), decrypt Master Key internally on USB key, transfer to new device secure enclave via CTAP2 protocol
Multiple recovery key management: create, enumerate, label, and revoke multiple USB recovery keys stored in different physical locations
Shamir's Secret Sharing backup: split Master Key backup into N shares with configurable threshold (recommended 5 shares, threshold 3), encrypt each share individually to its recipient
Shamir recovery: collect threshold number of shares from trusted parties, reconstruct Master Key backup, decrypt, and transfer to new device secure enclave
Post-recovery device re-pairing: after Master Key is restored to a new device, re-issue delegation tokens to existing devices or revoke stale delegations
Post-recovery key rotation: publish DNS key rotation record (_locd-rotate) with old and new public keys, invalidate all prior delegation tokens, enforce 7-day transition period
Irrecoverable identity handling: detect when no backup exists, guide user through new identity creation, provide per-service legacy credential recovery guidance
Recovery key health monitoring: periodic reminders to verify USB key accessibility, passphrase recall prompts, share holder reachability checks for Shamir setups
acceptance_criteria: Given a Master Key in a device secure enclave, when the user initiates USB recovery key creation with a valid passphrase and a connected FIDO2 USB key, then the encrypted Master Key is written to the USB key's resident credential storage and the operation completes without the private key ever existing in extractable form outside the enclave

Given an encrypted Master Key on a USB recovery key, when the user inserts the key into a new device, presses the physical presence button, and enters the correct passphrase, then the Master Key is decrypted internally and transferred to the new device's secure enclave via CTAP2

Given an encrypted Master Key on a USB recovery key, when the user enters an incorrect passphrase, then decryption fails, no key material is transferred, and the user is informed of the failure without revealing whether the passphrase or key is invalid

Given Argon2id parameters of 64MB memory, 3 iterations, and 4 parallelism, when encrypting the Master Key backup, then the implementation uses exactly these parameters and the resulting ciphertext is verifiably encrypted with XChaCha20-Poly1305

Given a user with multiple USB recovery keys, when one key is revoked, then the remaining keys continue to function independently for recovery and the revoked key cannot be used to restore the Master Key

Given a Shamir's Secret Sharing configuration of 5 shares with threshold 3, when 3 or more valid shares are collected, then the Master Key backup is correctly reconstructed, and when fewer than 3 shares are provided, then reconstruction fails completely with no partial information leakage
Given individually encrypted Shamir shares, when a share is delivered to a recipient, then only that specific recipient's key can decrypt their share and no other recipient's share

Given a successful Master Key recovery on a new device, when the user initiates device re-pairing, then new delegation tokens are issued from the recovered Master Key and all previous delegation tokens are invalidated

Given a successful Master Key recovery, when the key rotation record is published to DNS, then the record contains the old public key, new public key, timestamp, and a valid signature by the old key in the format v=locd-rotate1

Given a user whose Master Key device is lost and no recovery backup exists, when the user attempts recovery, then the system clearly communicates that the identity is permanently irrecoverable and offers guided new identity creation

Given a recovered identity with legacy bridge credentials synced to other mesh devices, when the old Master Key device is lost, then legacy credentials remain accessible on surviving mesh devices encrypted to their respective TPM-bound keys

Given a USB recovery key created on one platform (e.g., iOS), when recovery is performed on a different platform (e.g., Android), then the recovery succeeds provided both platforms support CTAP2 and Ed25519 in their secure enclaves
Given a recovery key creation flow, when the USB key is disconnected mid-write, then the operation fails atomically without leaving partial or corrupted credential data on the USB key

Given a post-recovery key rotation, when the 7-day transition period is active, then verifiers accept both old and new public keys for identity resolution, and after the transition period only the new key is accepted
technical_spec: Implement Argon2id key derivation with exactly 64MB memory cost, 3 time iterations, and 4 parallelism lanes as specified in the protocol; the derived key feeds into XChaCha20-Poly1305 for Master Key encryption

Use CTAP2 (Client to Authenticator Protocol v2.1) for all USB security key interactions including resident credential creation, physical presence verification, and secure key transfer to new device enclaves
FIDO2 resident credential storage must use the rk (resident key) extension to store the encrypted Master Key material directly on the USB authenticator, not on the host filesystem

Master Key export from secure enclave must use platform-specific secure export APIs (iOS: SecKeyCreateDecryptedData with kSecAttrTokenIDSecureEnclave; Android: KeyStore with setIsStrongBoxBacked) that ensure the raw private key is never exposed to userspace
Shamir's Secret Sharing implementation must use GF(2^8) arithmetic for share generation and Lagrange interpolation for reconstruction, ensuring information-theoretic security below the threshold

Each Shamir share must be individually encrypted to the recipient using their public key (Ed25519 converted to X25519 for Diffie-Hellman, then HKDF-SHA256 key derivation, then XChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption)
Recovery key metadata (creation timestamp, label, key identifier) must be stored in the user's identity record or local encrypted storage, but never the recovery key material itself
DNS key rotation record at _locd-rotate.<domain> must follow format: v=locd-rotate1; old=<old-pubkey>; new=<new-pubkey>; t=<timestamp>; sig=<signature-by-old-key> and must be DNSSEC-signed
Post-recovery delegation invalidation must revoke all existing delegation tokens by publishing their IDs to the _locd-revoke DNS record and the HTTPS revocation endpoint simultaneously
USB key physical presence verification must use the FIDO2 UP (User Presence) flag requiring a button press, preventing remote or automated recovery attempts
Recovery flow must implement a secure channel between USB key and new device secure enclave using CTAP2's pinUvAuthProtocol to prevent MITM during key transfer
Shamir share distribution should support offline delivery (QR codes, encrypted files) in addition to mesh-based delivery, since mesh may be unavailable during a recovery scenario

The system must handle partial failure during multi-key creation gracefully: if creating 3 USB recovery keys and the 2nd fails, the 1st remains valid and the 3rd is not attempted until the 2nd is resolved
Passphrase strength validation should enforce minimum entropy requirements but must not store or transmit the passphrase; it exists only in memory during the encryption/decryption operation
Recovery without backup must cleanly deauthorize the lost identity's cooperative namespace subdomain (if applicable) and release the username for re-registration with the new identity
Recovery System.api_contract: Core interfaces: RecoveryKeyManager with methods createUSBRecoveryKey(passphrase, usbKeyHandle) -> RecoveryKeyMetadata, recoverFromUSBKey(passphrase, usbKeyHandle, targetEnclaveHandle) -> MasterKeyReference, listRecoveryKeys() -> RecoveryKeyMetadata[], revokeRecoveryKey(keyId) -> void. ShamirManager with methods createShares(threshold, totalShares, recipientPublicKeys) -> EncryptedShare[], reconstructFromShares(shares, passphrase, targetEnclaveHandle) -> MasterKeyReference. PostRecoveryOrchestrator with methods rotateIdentity(oldMasterKey, newMasterKey, domain) -> RotationRecord, invalidateAllDelegations(masterKey, domain) -> RevocationRecord, repairDevices(masterKey, deviceKeys) -> DelegationToken[]. Backend API endpoints: POST /api/recovery/usb-key/create for initiating USB key backup, POST /api/recovery/usb-key/restore for initiating recovery flow, GET /api/recovery/keys for listing recovery key metadata, DELETE /api/recovery/keys/{keyId} for revoking a recovery key, POST /api/recovery/shamir/create-shares for generating Shamir shares, POST /api/recovery/shamir/reconstruct for threshold reconstruction, POST /api/recovery/rotate-identity for publishing DNS rotation records, POST /api/recovery/reauthorize-devices for post-recovery device re-pairing. All endpoints require active secure enclave session. USB key operations require CTAP2 transport layer. Shamir endpoints accept encrypted share blobs as base64url-encoded payloads.
dependencies: Hardware secure enclave access layer (iOS Secure Enclave / Android StrongBox / TPM 2.0) for Master Key export and import operations
CTAP2/FIDO2 client library for USB security key communication, resident credential management, and physical presence verification
Argon2id implementation with configurable memory, iteration, and parallelism parameters (e.g., libsodium or argon2-cffi)
HKDF-SHA256 key derivation for Shamir share encryption
Ed25519 signing for key rotation records and revocation statements (RFC 8032)
X25519 key agreement for encrypting Shamir shares to individual recipients (RFC 7748)
Shamir's Secret Sharing implementation over GF(2^8) with Lagrange interpolation
DNS management API or client for publishing _locd-rotate and _locd-revoke records with DNSSEC signing
Identity Layer module for Master Key public key retrieval and DNS record management
Delegation Layer module for issuing new delegation tokens post-recovery and revoking old ones
Revocation Layer module for publishing delegation revocations during key rotation
Mesh Connectivity Layer (optional) for syncing recovery metadata and Shamir shares between devices

Cooperative Namespace Service.summary: The Cooperative Namespace Service is a server-side system that operates the id.locd.net DNS zone, providing subdomain registration and identity record management for Loc'd users who do not own their own domain. It handles user registration with username allocation, publishes DNSSEC-signed TXT records at <username>._locd.id.locd.net containing Ed25519 public keys per the Loc'd identity format, manages revocation and key rotation records, supports seamless migration to user-owned domains, and is designed for federated operation by multiple independent organizations without vendor lock-in.
features: User registration with username validation and subdomain allocation under id.locd.net
DNS zone management for id.locd.net with automated DNSSEC signing of all records
Identity record publication as TXT records at <username>._locd.id.locd.net in v=locd1 format
Identity record updates authenticated by the user's Master Key signature
Revocation record management at _locd-revoke.<username>.id.locd.net for delegation ID revocation
Key rotation record management at _locd-rotate.<username>.id.locd.net for Master Key rotation
Domain migration workflow allowing users to move their identity to a self-owned domain
Federation API enabling multiple independent organizations to operate cooperative namespace nodes
Rate limiting and abuse prevention for registration and record update endpoints
Health monitoring and audit logging for all identity record lifecycle events
Username reservation and dispute resolution policy enforcement
TTL management with 300-second default for active records and 60-second TTL for revoked/expired records
acceptance_criteria: A new user can register a username and the system creates a valid TXT record at <username>._locd.id.locd.net within 60 seconds
Published TXT records conform exactly to the format: v=locd1; k=ed25519; p=<base64url-pubkey>; t=<unix-timestamp> with optional exp and rev fields
All DNS records served by the system pass DNSSEC validation when queried by a DNSSEC-validating resolver
Identity record updates require a valid Ed25519 signature from the Master Key matching the currently published public key
Unauthorized update attempts (wrong key, expired signature, replay) are rejected with appropriate error codes
Revocation records are published at _locd-revoke.<username>.id.locd.net within 30 seconds of a signed revocation request
Key rotation records are published at _locd-rotate.<username>.id.locd.net and old identity records are updated to the new key atomically
A user can initiate domain migration, receiving a signed migration token and instructions for publishing at their own domain
After successful migration verification, the cooperative namespace record is replaced with a CNAME or TXT redirect to the user's domain for a 30-day transition period
Usernames must be 3-63 characters, lowercase alphanumeric plus hyphens, not starting or ending with a hyphen, and not matching reserved names
The system rejects registration of usernames that are already taken, returning a 409 Conflict response
Active identity records are served with a 300-second TTL and revoked/expired records with a 60-second TTL
The federation sync API allows a secondary node to replicate the full zone state and serve authoritative DNS for id.locd.net
The system operates without requiring any proprietary client library or vendor-specific protocol extension
Registration and update endpoints enforce rate limits of no more than 10 registrations per IP per hour and 60 updates per user per hour
All API responses include no proprietary fields that would create vendor lock-in for clients
The system logs all record creation, update, revocation, rotation, and migration events with timestamps and requesting identity
technical_spec: DNS zone management via integration with a DNSSEC-capable authoritative DNS server (e.g., PowerDNS, BIND 9, or Knot DNS) with automated DNSKEY/RRSIG management
DNSSEC signing using ECDSA P-256 (algorithm 13) or Ed25519 (algorithm 15) for zone signing keys, with automated key rollover
REST API for user-facing operations (registration, update, revocation, rotation, migration) authenticated via Ed25519 challenge-response using the user's Master Key
Username validation regex: ^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]{1,61}[a-z0-9])?$ with a reserved names blocklist (admin, www, api, mail, locd, root, etc.)
Identity record format validation ensuring v=locd1, k=ed25519, p is valid 43-character base64url, t is a valid Unix timestamp, and optional exp/rev fields are well-formed
Record update authentication: client signs a canonical update payload (CBOR-encoded: username, new record fields, nonce, timestamp) with Master Key; server verifies against published public key
Key rotation workflow: accept rotation request signed by old Master Key containing old and new public keys, atomically update identity record and publish rotation record
Migration workflow: generate a migration challenge, user publishes a verification TXT record at _locd.<their-domain>, service verifies, then transitions cooperative record to redirect
Federation protocol: zone transfer (AXFR/IXFR) with TSIG authentication between authorized namespace operators, plus an HTTP-based sync API for record-level replication
Database storage for user accounts, record metadata, and audit logs using PostgreSQL with row-level security for multi-tenant federation
Rate limiting implemented at the API gateway level with per-IP and per-identity token bucket algorithms
Replay protection on all authenticated requests via server-issued nonces with a 60-second validity window and nonce deduplication store
Monitoring endpoints exposing zone health, DNSSEC signature validity remaining, record count, and federation sync status
Automated DNSSEC re-signing before signature expiry with alerting if re-signing fails
Revocation record aggregation: multiple revoked delegation IDs for a user are combined into a single _locd-revoke TXT record with comma-separated IDs per spec format
TXT record size management: if revocation list exceeds DNS TXT record size limits (255 bytes per string, multiple strings allowed), split across multiple strings within the record
Migration transition records maintained for 30 days with automated cleanup after transition period expires
Cooperative Namespace Service.api_contract: POST /v1/register {username, public_key, signature, timestamp} -> {status, domain, record} | POST /v1/identity/{username}/update {public_key, rev_url, exp, signature, nonce, timestamp} -> {status, record} | POST /v1/identity/{username}/revoke {delegation_ids[], signature, nonce, timestamp} -> {status, revocation_record} | POST /v1/identity/{username}/rotate {old_public_key, new_public_key, signature_by_old_key, nonce, timestamp} -> {status, rotation_record, new_identity_record} | POST /v1/identity/{username}/migrate/initiate {target_domain, signature, nonce, timestamp} -> {challenge_token, verification_instructions} | POST /v1/identity/{username}/migrate/verify {target_domain, signature, nonce, timestamp} -> {status, transition_record, transition_expires_at} | GET /v1/identity/{username} -> {username, domain, public_key, published_at, expires_at, rev_url, status} | GET /v1/health -> {status, zone_healthy, dnssec_valid, records_count, last_zone_update} | GET /v1/federation/zone -> AXFR-equivalent zone data (authenticated) | POST /v1/federation/sync {records[], source_node, tsig_signature} -> {status, accepted_count, rejected[]} | All authenticated endpoints require X-Locd-Signature header with Ed25519 signature over canonical request body, X-Locd-PublicKey header, and X-Locd-Nonce/X-Locd-Timestamp headers
dependencies: DNSSEC-capable authoritative DNS server (PowerDNS, BIND 9, or Knot DNS) for zone hosting and signing
PostgreSQL database for user accounts, record metadata, audit logs, and nonce deduplication
Ed25519 cryptographic library for signature verification (e.g., libsodium, PyNaCl, or ring)
CBOR library for parsing signed update payloads from clients (RFC 8949)
Domain registrar access for id.locd.net with DS record delegation for DNSSEC chain of trust
DNS zone update mechanism (RFC 2136 dynamic updates or direct database integration with PowerDNS)
TLS certificates for API endpoints (Let's Encrypt or equivalent)
Redis or equivalent for rate limiting token buckets and nonce deduplication cache
Monitoring stack for zone health, DNSSEC validity, and operational alerting

Service Discovery & Integration.summary: Service Discovery & Integration provides the service-side implementation of the Loc'd protocol, enabling services to advertise their Loc'd-native capabilities via DNS TXT records at _locd-svc.<service-domain>, implement the Verifier-side challenge-response authentication protocol from Section 7, and offer SDK libraries and middleware plugins for common web frameworks (Express, FastAPI, Go net/http, etc.) so that existing services can adopt Loc'd authentication with minimal code changes. This module is the primary integration surface for service operators who want to accept Loc'd identities alongside or in place of traditional authentication.
features: DNS service discovery record management: publish, update, and validate _locd-svc.<service-domain> TXT records with protocol version, coordination port, permitted actions list, and minimum delegation TTL
Service capability advertisement API: programmatic interface for services to declare supported actions, required delegation scopes, and minimum TTL constraints

Verifier-side challenge-response engine: full implementation of the Section 7 protocol including HELLO reception, DNS identity lookup with DNSSEC validation, CHALLENGE generation with cryptographic nonce, RESPONSE verification covering delegation chain validation, scope checking, revocation checking, and RESULT emission

Delegation token validation library: CBOR/COSE deserialization, Ed25519 signature verification against DNS-published Master Key, expiry checks, scope matching against service-declared actions, revocation list queries (DNS + HTTPS endpoint), and sub-delegation chain depth enforcement

Revocation checking subsystem: queries _locd-revoke.<domain> DNS records and optional HTTPS revocation endpoints declared in the identity record's rev field, with configurable caching respecting DNS TTL
Identity public key caching layer: caches resolved Master Key public keys for DNS TTL duration, respects delegation token expiry, and forces re-query on TTL expiration per Section 7.5
Express.js middleware plugin: drop-in middleware that intercepts requests, performs Loc'd verification, and populates request context with verified identity, delegation scope, and device key metadata
FastAPI middleware plugin: ASGI middleware performing Loc'd verification with dependency injection of verified identity into route handlers
Go net/http middleware plugin: http.Handler wrapper performing Loc'd verification and attaching identity to request context

Hybrid authentication support: middleware configuration to accept both Loc'd and legacy authentication (Bearer tokens, session cookies) during migration periods with dashboard indicator metadata (green/yellow/red per Section 10.5)
Action-based authorization decorators: route-level annotations that declare required Loc'd actions and automatically reject delegations lacking the required scope
WireGuard tunnel handoff: after successful verification, emit the service's WireGuard public key in RESULT and accept the claimant's WireGuard key to establish an encrypted tunnel per Section 9.2
Service registration CLI tool: command-line utility for operators to generate, validate, and publish _locd-svc DNS records with dry-run and DNSSEC pre-validation
Protocol negotiation handling: respond with version_unsupported and supported versions list when receiving incompatible HELLO messages per Section 13.2
acceptance_criteria: A _locd-svc TXT record published via the SDK contains all required fields (v=locd-svc1, port, actions, min-delegation-ttl) and passes DNS TXT record format validation

The Verifier engine correctly verifies a valid HELLO/CHALLENGE/RESPONSE flow against a claimant with a valid delegation token signed by the Master Key published in DNS, returning RESULT with verified=true
The Verifier engine rejects a claimant whose delegation token has expired (expires_at in the past) and returns reason code delegation_expired
The Verifier engine rejects a claimant whose delegation token's delegation_id appears in the _locd-revoke DNS record and returns reason code delegation_revoked
The Verifier engine rejects a claimant whose delegation token's delegation_id appears in the HTTPS revocation list at the rev URL and returns reason code delegation_revoked
The Verifier engine rejects a claimant whose delegation token does not include the service's domain in the services array and returns reason code scope_violation
The Verifier engine rejects a claimant attempting an action not listed in the delegation token's actions array and returns reason code scope_violation
The Verifier engine rejects identity lookups that fail DNSSEC validation and returns reason code dnssec_invalid
The Verifier engine rejects RESPONSE messages where the nonce in the signature does not match the issued CHALLENGE nonce, returning reason code nonce_mismatch
The Verifier engine rejects RESPONSE messages with timestamp skew exceeding 60 seconds and returns reason code timestamp_skew
The Verifier engine rejects sub-delegation chains exceeding depth 3 and returns reason code chain_too_deep
The Verifier engine enforces min-delegation-ttl: delegation tokens with remaining TTL below the service's declared minimum are rejected with reason code delegation_expired
The identity public key cache stores resolved keys for exactly the DNS TTL duration and re-queries DNS after TTL expiration
The identity public key cache does not serve cached keys past a delegation token's expires_at timestamp
Express.js middleware returns HTTP 401 with a JSON body containing the Loc'd reason code when verification fails
Express.js middleware populates req.locd with identity domain, device key, delegation scope, and delegation expiry on successful verification
FastAPI middleware raises HTTPException(401) with Loc'd reason code detail when verification fails
FastAPI middleware injects a LocdIdentity dependency with domain, device_key, scoped_actions, and delegation_expiry into verified route handlers
Go middleware sets locd.Identity in request context on success and returns 401 with reason code JSON on failure
Hybrid auth mode accepts both Loc'd-verified and Bearer-token-authenticated requests on the same endpoint, with Loc'd taking precedence when both are present
Action-based authorization decorator rejects requests where the verified delegation's actions array does not include the route's declared required action
After successful verification, the RESULT message includes the service's WireGuard public key as a byte string and the service accepts the claimant's WireGuard key to configure a peer entry
The CLI tool validates a _locd-svc record against the protocol format and exits with error code 1 and descriptive message if any required field is missing or malformed
The CLI tool performs a dry-run DNS publish that resolves the record locally without modifying DNS and reports validation success or failure
Protocol negotiation returns version_unsupported with a list of supported versions when receiving a HELLO with an unsupported protocol version
All CBOR message serialization and deserialization round-trips without data loss for all message types (HELLO, CHALLENGE, RESPONSE, RESULT)
The Verifier engine handles DNS lookup failure gracefully by serving cached identity if available (degraded mode) or returning dns_lookup_failed if no cache exists
Concurrent verification of 100 simultaneous claimants completes without deadlocks, race conditions, or incorrect verification results
technical_spec: Implement CBOR encoding/decoding per RFC 8949 for all wire format messages (HELLO, CHALLENGE, RESPONSE, RESULT) using established libraries (cbor2 for Python, cbor-x for Node.js, fxamacker/cbor for Go)
Implement COSE Sign1 verification per RFC 9052 for delegation token signature validation, specifically algorithm -8 (EdDSA) with Ed25519 keys
Ed25519 signature verification per RFC 8032 for both delegation token validation (Master Key signs token) and challenge-response validation (Device Key signs nonce || timestamp || verifier_domain)
DNS TXT record parsing must handle the semicolon-delimited key-value format: v=locd-svc1; port=<uint16>; actions=<comma-separated-strings>; min-delegation-ttl=<uint32>
DNSSEC validation chain must be verified for all _locd and _locd-revoke lookups; use system resolver with DNSSEC-aware flags (AD bit) or dedicated DNSSEC validation library
DNS-over-HTTPS (RFC 8484) support for identity lookups to prevent network observers from correlating verification targets
Nonce generation must use cryptographically secure random bytes (32 bytes) per challenge to prevent replay attacks
Timestamp tolerance window is 60 seconds in either direction per Section 7; reject any message outside this window
Identity public key cache must be keyed by domain name, store the DNS TTL as expiration, and implement atomic read/write to support concurrent verifications
Revocation checking must query both DNS (_locd-revoke.<domain>) and HTTPS (rev field URL) endpoints; a match in either source constitutes revocation
HTTPS revocation list response must be validated: verify the Ed25519 signature in the signature field against the identity's Master Key before trusting revocation entries
Sub-delegation chain validation must verify each link in the chain (root-first ordering), confirm no scope expansion at any level, confirm no expiry extension, and enforce max chain depth of 3

WireGuard key exchange after verification: service generates or retrieves its WireGuard X25519 public key and includes it in the RESULT message; claimant's WireGuard key is received in a subsequent message
min-delegation-ttl enforcement: compare (delegation.expires_at - current_time) against the service's declared minimum; reject if remaining TTL is insufficient
Middleware must be transport-agnostic at the verification core: the same verification engine must work whether the CBOR messages arrive over raw TCP, WebSocket, or HTTP
Length-prefixed framing per Section 13: 4-byte big-endian uint32 length prefix followed by CBOR payload for raw TCP transport

Service discovery record must be publishable via standard DNS update mechanisms (nsupdate, cloud provider APIs) and the CLI tool must support at least manual zone file generation and Route53/Cloudflare API integration
Graceful degradation when DNS is unreachable: serve cached identity keys with a degraded trust indicator; log the degradation event for operator alerting
Thread-safe / async-safe verification pipeline: the verification engine must support concurrent verifications without shared mutable state beyond the cache layer
Middleware must not block the event loop (Node.js) or request thread (Python sync frameworks); DNS lookups and revocation checks must be async or performed in worker threads
Error responses must use the exact reason codes from Section 7.4 to ensure interoperability across implementations
Logging must capture verification events (success/failure with reason code, identity domain, delegation ID, timestamp) without logging private keys or full delegation token contents
Service Discovery & Integration.api_contract: Core SDK Interface: LocdVerifier class with methods: constructor(config: {serviceDomain, wireguardPublicKey, actions[], minDelegationTTL, dnssecResolver?, revocationCacheSeconds?}), verify(helloMessage) -> {verified: bool, reasonCode: string, identity?: {domain, deviceKey, delegationScope, delegationExpiry}, wireguardKey?: bytes}, publishServiceRecord(dnsProvider, record) -> {success, recordValue}, revokeCheck(identityDomain, delegationId) -> {revoked: bool, reason?}. DNS Record Format: _locd-svc.<service-domain> TXT 'v=locd-svc1; port=<uint16>; actions=<csv>; min-delegation-ttl=<seconds>'. Express Middleware: app.use(locdAuth({serviceDomain, actions, fallbackAuth?})) populating req.locd. FastAPI Middleware: app.add_middleware(LocdAuthMiddleware, service_domain=, actions=) with Depends(get_locd_identity). Go Middleware: locd.Middleware(locd.Config{ServiceDomain, Actions}) returning http.Handler. CLI: locd-svc publish --domain <domain> --port <port> --actions <csv> --min-ttl <seconds> [--provider route53|cloudflare|zonefile] [--dry-run]
dependencies: Identity Layer (Section 5): DNS TXT record format for _locd.<domain> defines how the Verifier resolves claimant Master Key public keys
Delegation Layer (Section 6): CBOR/COSE delegation token format and signing rules define what this module validates
Verification Layer (Section 7): challenge-response protocol messages (HELLO, CHALLENGE, RESPONSE, RESULT) and reason codes define the wire protocol this module implements
Revocation Layer (Section 8): DNS revocation records and HTTPS revocation list format define what this module checks during verification
Mesh Connectivity Layer (Section 9): WireGuard tunnel establishment after verification defines the post-auth handoff this module initiates
Ed25519 cryptographic library for signature verification (libsodium, tweetnacl, or platform-native)
CBOR serialization library (cbor2 for Python, cbor-x for Node.js, fxamacker/cbor for Go)
COSE library for Sign1 structure parsing and verification
DNSSEC-validating DNS resolver or library (unbound, getdns, or trust-dns)
DNS-over-HTTPS client library for privacy-preserving identity lookups
WireGuard userspace library or system integration for key generation and peer configuration
HTTP client library for fetching HTTPS revocation lists from rev endpoints

Security Monitoring & Audit.summary: Security Monitoring & Audit provides enterprise-tier security hardening, monitoring, and audit infrastructure for the Loc'd protocol. It implements comprehensive audit logging across all protocol operations (key generation, delegation lifecycle, verification attempts, mesh establishment, credential access, recovery), real-time threat detection for anomalous patterns (rapid delegation creation, verification failures from unusual sources, replay attempts, scope escalation), security event alerting, compliance reporting, rate limiting and DoS protection for verification endpoints, nonce tracking with a replay detection database, and performance monitoring for cryptographic operations and DNS lookups. This module is observability-only and does not modify core protocol logic.
features: Structured audit log ingestion for all protocol events: key generation, delegation creation/validation/revocation, verification attempts with reason codes, mesh tunnel establishment/teardown, legacy bridge credential access, and recovery operations
Tamper-evident append-only audit log storage with cryptographic chaining (each entry references the hash of the previous entry) to detect log manipulation

Real-time threat detection engine analyzing event streams for anomalous patterns: rapid delegation creation from a single master key, burst verification failures from a single source, timestamp skew anomalies, replay attempt detection, and delegation scope escalation attempts
Configurable security alerting and notification system supporting webhook, email, and in-protocol push channels with severity levels (info, warning, critical) and deduplication windows

Compliance reporting dashboard and API for delegation usage statistics, revocation response time tracking (time from revocation request to DNS propagation and HTTPS list update), key rotation schedule adherence, and delegation expiry distribution analysis
Rate limiting engine for verification endpoints with configurable per-identity and per-IP thresholds, sliding window counters, and graduated response (throttle then block)
Nonce tracking database for replay detection with automatic expiry-based cleanup, supporting the 60-second timestamp tolerance window defined in the protocol

Performance monitoring for cryptographic operations (Ed25519 sign/verify latency, X25519 key agreement, CBOR encode/decode, COSE Sign1 operations) and DNS lookup timing (DNSSEC validation, DoH round-trip, cache hit rates)

Event correlation engine that links related events across protocol layers (e.g., a delegation creation followed by immediate verification attempts across many services indicates potential credential stuffing with a stolen delegation)
Audit log query and export API supporting time-range queries, filtering by event type/identity/device/outcome, and export in JSON Lines and CSV formats for external SIEM integration
acceptance_criteria: Every key generation event (master, device, session) produces an audit log entry containing: event type, timestamp, identity domain, key fingerprint (public key hash), hardware attestation status, and result (success/failure with error code)

Every delegation creation produces an audit log entry containing: delegator identity, delegate key fingerprint, delegation ID, scope (services and actions arrays), expiry, sub-delegation flag, and the signing master key fingerprint

Every verification attempt produces an audit log entry containing: claimant identity domain, verifier identity domain, device key fingerprint, delegation ID, each verification step result (DNS lookup, DNSSEC validation, delegation signature, expiry check, revocation check, scope check, nonce verification, timestamp check), final outcome (verified/rejected), and reason code from the protocol's reason code table

Every revocation event produces an audit log entry containing: revoking identity, delegation ID revoked, revocation reason (device_lost/device_compromised/key_rotation/scope_change/user_initiated), publication targets (DNS and/or HTTPS), and timestamp

Audit log entries are cryptographically chained: each entry includes a SHA-256 hash of the previous entry, and breaking the chain (inserting, deleting, or modifying an entry) is detectable by a verification scan that completes in under 10 seconds for 1 million entries
The threat detection engine generates an alert within 5 seconds when a single master key creates more than 10 delegations within a 1-minute window
The threat detection engine generates an alert within 5 seconds when a single identity receives more than 50 verification failures within a 5-minute window

The threat detection engine detects and alerts on replay attempts: when a previously-seen nonce is resubmitted within the 60-second tolerance window, the attempt is flagged and the source IP and identity are recorded

The threat detection engine detects delegation scope escalation attempts: when a sub-delegation token contains services or actions not present in the parent delegation, an alert is generated with the parent and child delegation IDs

Rate limiting blocks a source IP after it exceeds the configured verification request threshold (default: 100 requests per minute per IP) and returns a rate_limited reason code, resuming service after the sliding window expires
Rate limiting applies per-identity throttling (default: 20 verification requests per minute per identity) independently from per-IP limits, and the more restrictive limit takes effect

The nonce tracking database stores nonces for exactly 120 seconds (2x the 60-second timestamp tolerance) and automatically purges expired entries, maintaining lookup latency under 1 millisecond at 10,000 concurrent verifications per second

Compliance reports accurately calculate: average revocation response time (time between revocation request and confirmation of DNS record update), percentage of delegations using default 24-hour expiry vs custom expiry, number of active vs expired vs revoked delegations per identity, and key rotation frequency per identity

Performance monitoring records p50, p95, and p99 latencies for: Ed25519 signature generation, Ed25519 signature verification, DNSSEC validation, DoH lookup round-trip, CBOR encoding/decoding, and COSE Sign1 creation/verification

Audit log query API returns results within 2 seconds for time-range queries spanning up to 30 days across up to 10 million log entries, with filtering by event type, identity, device key fingerprint, and outcome
Audit log export produces valid JSON Lines output where each line is a self-contained JSON object parseable by standard SIEM tools (Splunk, Elasticsearch, Datadog)

Alert notifications are delivered to configured webhook endpoints within 10 seconds of threat detection, with JSON payloads containing: alert ID, severity, threat type, affected identities, evidence summary, and recommended action
All monitoring and audit components operate without modifying protocol message formats or verification logic; they consume events emitted by core protocol components via an internal event bus

The system handles graceful degradation: if the audit log storage is unavailable, protocol operations continue but emit warnings; events are buffered in memory (configurable max buffer size, default 10,000 events) and flushed when storage recovers
technical_spec: Audit log entries use a structured schema with required fields: event_id (UUID v4), event_type (enum: key_generation, delegation_created, delegation_validated, delegation_revoked, verification_attempt, verification_success, verification_failure, mesh_established, mesh_teardown, credential_access, recovery_initiated, recovery_completed), timestamp (Unix microseconds), identity_domain, and prev_hash (SHA-256 of previous entry bytes for tamper detection chain)

Audit log storage backend is abstracted behind a LogStore interface supporting append, query-by-time-range, query-by-filter, and verify-chain operations; default implementation uses append-only file storage with periodic rotation (one file per hour) and optional SQLite index for query acceleration

Threat detection rules are defined as configurable policies with parameters: event pattern (event type + conditions), time window, threshold count, severity level, and alert action; default rules cover rapid delegation (>10 in 1 min), verification failure burst (>50 in 5 min), replay detection (duplicate nonce within tolerance), and scope escalation (sub-delegation exceeds parent scope)

The threat detection engine uses sliding window counters implemented with a time-bucketed ring buffer (1-second granularity, configurable window size) to minimize memory usage while maintaining accurate counts; estimated memory: 1KB per tracked identity/IP pair

Rate limiting uses a token bucket algorithm with configurable parameters per rate limit rule: bucket capacity, refill rate, and refill interval; state is stored in a shared-nothing per-worker hash map with LRU eviction when memory threshold is reached (default: 100MB)

Nonce tracking uses a time-partitioned hash set: nonces are stored in 10-second buckets, entire buckets are dropped after 120 seconds; this avoids per-entry expiry timers and supports O(1) lookup and O(1) amortized cleanup

Performance metrics are collected using a lockless histogram implementation (HDR Histogram or equivalent) with microsecond precision, supporting p50/p95/p99/max queries without blocking the hot path; metrics are exposed via a pull-based /metrics endpoint in Prometheus exposition format

The internal event bus uses an async broadcast channel pattern where core protocol components emit events and monitoring subscribers consume them; events are fire-and-forget from the protocol's perspective to ensure zero latency impact on verification operations

Alert deduplication uses a (threat_type, identity_domain, source_ip) composite key with a configurable suppression window (default: 5 minutes); duplicate alerts within the window increment a counter on the original alert rather than generating new notifications

Compliance report generation queries the audit log with aggregation pipelines: delegation lifecycle analysis joins delegation_created events with delegation_revoked events by delegation_id to compute active duration and revocation response time; key rotation analysis groups key_generation events by identity_domain with time-gap detection

DNS lookup performance monitoring tracks three distinct phases separately: DoH TCP connection time, DNS query round-trip time, and DNSSEC validation computation time; cache hit/miss ratio is tracked per identity domain with 5-minute reporting intervals
Audit log query API supports cursor-based pagination with opaque continuation tokens to handle large result sets without offset-based performance degradation; maximum page size is 1000 entries

Log export format for SIEM integration uses JSON Lines (one JSON object per line) with a standardized field mapping: timestamp in ISO 8601, event_type as string, outcome as string, and all protocol-specific fields nested under a details object to avoid top-level field conflicts with SIEM schemas

Replay detection integrates with the verification layer by intercepting the RESPONSE message before final verification: the nonce from the CHALLENGE is checked against the nonce tracking database, and if found, the verification is rejected with reason code nonce_mismatch and a replay_detected threat event is emitted

All monitoring data (metrics, alert state, rate limit counters) is ephemeral and reconstructable; only the audit log requires persistent storage; this means monitoring components can restart without data loss concerns beyond a brief gap in real-time detection

Cryptographic chain verification for audit logs uses incremental verification: a background process continuously validates the chain as entries are appended, maintaining a verified_up_to cursor; full re-verification is only needed after process restart or suspected tampering
Security Monitoring & Audit.api_contract: Core interfaces: (1) EventBus - async broadcast channel with emit(AuditEvent) for protocol components and subscribe(filter) for monitoring consumers; (2) AuditLogger - append(event) -> event_id, query(time_range, filters, cursor, limit) -> Page<AuditEvent>, verify_chain(start, end) -> ChainVerification, export(time_range, filters, format) -> Stream<bytes>; (3) ThreatDetector - register_rule(ThreatRule), on_event(AuditEvent) internally consumed, get_active_alerts(filters) -> list of Alert; (4) RateLimiter - check(identity, source_ip) -> RateLimitResult with allowed boolean and retry_after seconds; (5) NonceTracker - record(nonce, timestamp) -> bool indicating if nonce was already seen, cleanup() called automatically; (6) MetricsCollector - record_timing(operation, duration_us), get_metrics() -> MetricsSnapshot with histograms per operation; HTTP endpoints: GET /api/audit/events?start=&end=&type=&identity=&outcome=&cursor=&limit= for log queries, GET /api/audit/export?start=&end=&format=jsonl|csv for bulk export, GET /api/audit/chain/verify?start=&end= for integrity verification, GET /api/monitoring/alerts?severity=&active= for current alerts, GET /api/monitoring/metrics in Prometheus format, GET /api/monitoring/compliance/report?period= for compliance summaries, POST /api/monitoring/rules for threat rule management, GET /api/monitoring/rate-limits/status for rate limit state inspection
dependencies: Identity Layer (Section 5) - required for resolving identity domains referenced in audit events and for DNSSEC validation monitoring
Delegation Layer (Section 6) - required for intercepting delegation creation/validation events and for scope escalation detection in sub-delegations
Verification Layer (Section 7) - required for intercepting verification attempts and results, challenge-response monitoring, and nonce replay detection integration
Revocation Layer (Section 8) - required for tracking revocation events, measuring revocation response times, and monitoring DNS/HTTPS revocation list propagation
Mesh Connectivity Layer (Section 9) - required for monitoring tunnel establishment/teardown events and mesh sync operations
Legacy Bridge Layer (Section 10) - required for logging credential access events without exposing credential contents
DNS infrastructure with DNSSEC (Sections 5.3, 12) - required for monitoring DNS lookup performance and DNSSEC validation timing
CBOR encoding library (RFC 8949) - required for parsing protocol messages in the event stream for detailed audit logging
Cryptographic primitives: SHA-256 for audit log chaining, Ed25519 for signed compliance reports and revocation list signature verification

tasks.title: Project scaffolding and build system setup
tasks.description: Initialize the module with proper directory structure, Cargo.toml configuration for Rust or equivalent, CI/CD pipelines, and dependency management. Set up workspace structure with separate crates for core crypto, HAL, and platform backends. Configure cross-compilation targets for iOS, Android, and Linux. Include libsodium-sys, tpm2-tss, and platform SDK dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: Build system compiles for all three target platforms

tasks.title: Define core error type hierarchy
tasks.description: Implement the structured error hierarchy: HardwareError (Unavailable, Locked, AttestationFailed), AuthError (BiometricFailed, PinRequired, Cancelled), CryptoError (VerificationFailed, InvalidKey, InvalidInput, NonceReuse), KeyError (NotFound, AlreadyExists, Expired). Each error type must implement Display, Debug, Error traits, and support error chaining. Include conversion implementations from underlying library errors.
acceptance_criteria: All error types defined per specification

tasks.title: Define key type enumerations and capabilities
tasks.description: Create KeyType enum distinguishing MasterKey (Tier 1, Ed25519, biometric-gated), DeviceKey (Tier 2, Ed25519, TPM-bound), and SessionKey (Tier 3, X25519, memory-only). Define AuthPolicy enum {BiometricRequired, DeviceUnlock, None}, HsmCapabilities struct with platform, supports_attestation, supports_biometric, max_key_count fields. Implement serialization/deserialization for these types.

tasks.title: Implement Base64url encoding/decoding (no padding)
tasks.description: Implement RFC 4648 Base64url encoding and decoding without padding as required by protocol identity format. Functions: base64url_encode(data: bytes) -> string producing exactly 43 characters for 32-byte input; base64url_decode(string) -> bytes. Must handle edge cases, reject invalid characters, validate length constraints. Include constant-time operations where security critical.

tasks.title: Implement cryptographically secure random number generation
tasks.description: Create secure_random(length: int) -> bytes function using platform CSPRNG: SecRandomCopyBytes on iOS, SecureRandom on Android, getrandom() on Linux. Must handle errors gracefully, ensure proper seeding, and never fall back to insecure RNG. Include tests for entropy quality and error handling paths.
acceptance_criteria: Handles errors without fallback to insecure RNG

tasks.title: Implement HKDF-SHA256 key derivation (extract phase)
tasks.description: Implement HKDF-SHA256 extract phase per RFC 5869. Function takes input key material (IKM) and salt, produces pseudorandom key (PRK). Must handle edge cases: empty salt defaults to hash-length zeros, variable-length IKM. Use platform SHA-256 implementation or ring/openssl. Include test vectors from RFC 5869.
acceptance_criteria: Extract phase produces correct PRK for test vectors

tasks.title: Implement HKDF-SHA256 key derivation (expand phase)
tasks.description: Implement HKDF-SHA256 expand phase per RFC 5869. Function takes PRK, info string, and output length. Supports domain-separated keys with info strings like 'locd-credential-encryption' vs 'locd-session-key'. Must validate output length constraints (<= 255 * hash length). Include full RFC 5869 test vectors.
acceptance_criteria: Expand phase matches RFC 5869 test vectors

tasks.title: Implement HKDF-SHA256 complete key derivation API
tasks.description: Create unified hkdf_sha256(ikm: bytes, salt: bytes, info: bytes, length: int) -> bytes function combining extract and expand phases. Add convenience wrappers for common use cases: derive_credential_encryption_key, derive_session_key. Document security considerations, salt generation requirements, and info string conventions.
acceptance_criteria: Complete HKDF matches RFC 5869 test vectors

tasks.title: Implement Argon2id password hashing core
tasks.description: Implement Argon2id password hashing with FIXED parameters: 64MB memory cost, 3 iterations, 4-way parallelism per protocol Section 11.1. These parameters MUST NOT be configurable to prevent weakening. Function: argon2id_hash(passphrase: bytes, salt: bytes[16]) -> bytes[32]. Use libsodium or argon2 crate. Validate salt length strictly.
acceptance_criteria: Timing is measurably longer than reduced parameters

tasks.title: Implement Ed25519 signature verification (RFC 8032)
tasks.description: Implement pure cryptographic Ed25519 signature verification per RFC 8032. Function: verify_ed25519(public_key: bytes[32], message: bytes, signature: bytes[64]) -> bool. Must include strict canonical encoding checks on deserialized signatures and public keys to prevent malleability attacks. Use libsodium or ed25519-dalek. Include test vectors from RFC 8032.
acceptance_criteria: Single bit flip in message/signature/key causes rejection

tasks.title: Implement X25519 key agreement (RFC 7748)
tasks.description: Implement X25519 ECDH key agreement per RFC 7748. Function: x25519_agree(our_secret: KeyHandle, their_public: bytes[32]) -> bytes[32]. Must reject low-order points to prevent small-subgroup attacks. Use libsodium or x25519-dalek. Validate that two independently generated key pairs produce identical 32-byte shared secrets.
acceptance_criteria: Key agreement produces identical secrets on both sides
Invalid public keys return structured errors

tasks.title: Implement ChaCha20-Poly1305 symmetric encryption
tasks.description: Implement IETF ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD encryption per RFC 8439 with 12-byte nonces. Function: encrypt_chacha20poly1305(key: bytes[32], nonce: bytes[12], plaintext: bytes, aad: bytes) -> bytes. Returns ciphertext with 16-byte Poly1305 tag appended. Use libsodium or RustCrypto chacha20poly1305 crate.
acceptance_criteria: Same inputs produce different outputs with different nonces

tasks.title: Implement ChaCha20-Poly1305 symmetric decryption
tasks.description: Implement ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD decryption with authentication verification. Function: decrypt_chacha20poly1305(key: bytes[32], nonce: bytes[12], ciphertext: bytes, aad: bytes) -> Result<bytes>. Must return structured error for authentication failure, not generic exception. Verify constant-time tag comparison to prevent timing attacks.
acceptance_criteria: Valid ciphertext decrypts to original plaintext

tasks.title: Implement XChaCha20-Poly1305 symmetric encryption
tasks.description: Implement XChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD with 24-byte nonces and HChaCha20 subkey derivation for Legacy Bridge credential storage and USB Recovery Key encryption. Function: encrypt_xchacha20poly1305(key: bytes[32], nonce: bytes[24], plaintext: bytes, aad: bytes) -> bytes. Uses libsodium or implement HChaCha20 subkey derivation.

tasks.title: Implement XChaCha20-Poly1305 symmetric decryption
tasks.description: Implement XChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD decryption. Function: decrypt_xchacha20poly1305(key: bytes[32], nonce: bytes[24], ciphertext: bytes, aad: bytes) -> Result<bytes>. Must reject tampered input with structured errors. Verify HChaCha20 subkey derivation matches encryption side.

tasks.title: Define Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL) trait interface
tasks.description: Define the unified HAL trait/interface with platform-agnostic methods: detect() -> HsmCapabilities; generate_key(key_type: KeyType, label: string, auth_policy: AuthPolicy) -> KeyHandle; sign(handle: KeyHandle, message: bytes, auth_context: AuthContext) -> bytes[64]; get_public_key(handle: KeyHandle) -> bytes[32]; generate_attestation(handle: KeyHandle) -> AttestationQuote; delete_key(handle: KeyHandle) -> void; key_exists(handle: KeyHandle) -> bool; list_keys(key_type: KeyType) -> [KeyHandle]. Include KeyHandle opaque reference design.
acceptance_criteria: HAL trait defines all required methods
Trait is object-safe for dynamic dispatch

tasks.title: Design KeyHandle opaque reference system
tasks.description: Design KeyHandle to be an opaque reference that never exposes private key material. Platform backends map handles to hardware-specific identifiers: Keychain reference on iOS, alias string on Android, persistent TPM handle on Linux. Implement handle validation, lifetime management, and secure cleanup. Include handle serialization for persistence (without key material).
acceptance_criteria: KeyHandle contains no private key material

tasks.title: Define AttestationQuote structure and formats
tasks.description: Create AttestationQuote struct {format: string, data: bytes, certificate_chain: [bytes]} that wraps platform-specific attestation data in normalized envelope. Support TPM2B_ATTEST for TPM, DeviceCheck attestation for iOS, Key Attestation certificate chain for Android. Include format version field for future extensibility.
acceptance_criteria: AttestationQuote supports all three platform formats

tasks.title: Implement HAL platform detection logic
tasks.description: Implement detect() function that determines available hardware security module type at runtime. Check for TPM 2.0 on Linux (via /dev/tpmrm0 or tpm2-tss probing), Secure Enclave on iOS (always available on supported devices), StrongBox/TEE on Android (via Android Keystore API feature detection). Return HsmCapabilities with accurate feature flags.
acceptance_criteria: Correctly detects Secure Enclave on iOS
Returns unsupported-platform error when no HSM available

tasks.title: Implement iOS Secure Enclave HAL backend - scaffolding
tasks.description: Create iOS Secure Enclave backend module structure with FFI bindings to Security.framework and LocalAuthentication.framework. Set up conditional compilation for iOS target. Define internal mappings from KeyHandle to SecKeyRef. Implement basic connectivity test to verify Secure Enclave accessibility.

tasks.title: Implement iOS Secure Enclave key generation
tasks.description: Implement generate_key for iOS using Security.framework with kSecAttrTokenIDSecureEnclave. Support MasterKey (Tier 1) and DeviceKey (Tier 2) generation with appropriate key attributes. Apply biometric gating policy using kSecAttrAccessControl with appropriate SecAccessControlCreateFlags. Store key reference in iOS Keychain with proper access constraints.
acceptance_criteria: Ed25519 keys generated in Secure Enclave
Keys have correct key type attributes
Biometric policy applied per key type

tasks.title: Implement iOS Secure Enclave signing with biometric gating
tasks.description: Implement sign() for iOS using SecKeyCreateSignature with LAContext for biometric gating. For MasterKey (Tier 1), require biometric/PIN authentication per signing operation using LocalAuthentication.framework. For DeviceKey (Tier 2), no re-authentication required after key creation. Handle biometric failure cases (cancelled, failed, not enrolled).
acceptance_criteria: MasterKey requires biometric auth per operation

tasks.title: Implement iOS Secure Enclave public key extraction
tasks.description: Implement get_public_key() for iOS using SecKeyCopyExternalRepresentation to extract the 32-byte public key from a Secure Enclave key reference. Validate public key format and ensure it matches Ed25519 specification. Cache public keys where appropriate for performance.

tasks.title: Implement iOS DeviceCheck attestation generation
tasks.description: Implement generate_attestation() for iOS using DeviceCheck DCAppAttestService. Generate attestation for Device Key that can be included in delegation token field 10. Handle attestation service availability (iOS 14.0+). Format attestation data in AttestationQuote envelope.
acceptance_criteria: Attestation can be validated by remote verifier
Unsupported iOS versions return appropriate error

tasks.title: Implement iOS Secure Enclave key management operations
tasks.description: Implement delete_key(), key_exists(), and list_keys() for iOS. delete_key removes key from Keychain. key_exists checks Keychain without loading key material. list_keys enumerates keys by KeyType filter. Handle Keychain access errors and return structured KeyError types.

tasks.title: Implement Android StrongBox HAL backend - scaffolding
tasks.description: Create Android StrongBox/TEE backend module with JNI bindings to Android Keystore API and BiometricPrompt API. Set up conditional compilation for Android target. Define internal mappings from KeyHandle to keystore alias. Test StrongBox availability on target devices.
acceptance_criteria: JNI bindings to Keystore API established

tasks.title: Implement Android StrongBox key generation
tasks.description: Implement generate_key for Android using Android Keystore with setIsStrongBoxBacked(true). Support MasterKey (Tier 1) with setUserAuthenticationRequired(true) and setInvalidatedByBiometricEnrollment(false). Support DeviceKey (Tier 2) without user authentication requirement. Use KeyGenParameterSpec.Builder with appropriate purposes.

tasks.title: Implement Android StrongBox signing with biometric gating
tasks.description: Implement sign() for Android using Keystore signing with BiometricPrompt for MasterKey operations. For Tier 1 keys, show BiometricPrompt before signing. For Tier 2 keys, sign directly. Handle biometric errors (ERROR_CANCELED, ERROR_LOCKOUT, ERROR_NO_BIOMETRICS). Use Signature.initSign() with KeyStore.PrivateKeyEntry.

tasks.title: Implement Android StrongBox public key extraction
tasks.description: Implement get_public_key() for Android using KeyStore.getCertificate() and extracting the 32-byte Ed25519 public key from the certificate or key entry. Validate public key format. Handle cases where key exists but certificate is not available.

tasks.title: Implement Android Key Attestation generation
tasks.description: Implement generate_attestation() for Android using KeyGenParameterSpec.setAttestationChallenge() and retrieving attestation certificate chain. Format attestation in AttestationQuote envelope per protocol. Support hardware-backed attestation when available. Validate certificate chain structure.
acceptance_criteria: Chain includes hardware attestation when available
Can be validated by remote verifier

tasks.title: Implement Android StrongBox key management operations
tasks.description: Implement delete_key(), key_exists(), and list_keys() for Android. delete_key uses KeyStore.deleteEntry(). key_exists checks aliases. list_keys enumerates entries filtered by KeyType. Handle Android Keystore exceptions and map to structured KeyError types.

tasks.title: Implement Linux TPM 2.0 HAL backend - scaffolding
tasks.description: Create Linux TPM 2.0 backend using tpm2-tss library (ESAPI). Set up bindings to Esys_ functions. Implement TPM device discovery (/dev/tpmrm0, /dev/tpm0). Test TPM 2.0 availability and version. Define internal mapping from KeyHandle to TPM persistent handle.

tasks.title: Implement Linux TPM 2.0 context and resource management
tasks.description: Implement TPM context initialization with Esys_Initialize(), proper TCTI configuration, and resource manager integration. Implement cleanup with Esys_Finalize() and proper handle flushing. Handle TPM resource limits and implement automatic session management. Include error handling for TPM unavailable/locked states.

tasks.title: Implement Linux TPM 2.0 key generation
tasks.description: Implement generate_key for TPM 2.0 using Esys_Create() and Esys_EvictControl() to make keys persistent. Generate Ed25519 keys (or ECDSA if Ed25519 not supported, with clear documentation). Set appropriate authPolicy for key types. Store persistent handles for key retrieval.

tasks.title: Implement Linux TPM 2.0 signing operations
tasks.description: Implement sign() for TPM 2.0 using Esys_Sign() with proper authorization sessions. Handle policy sessions for authenticated keys. For Tier 1 keys, implement authorization callback (may require OS-level integration). For Tier 2, use standard authorization. Handle TPM lockout and auth failure scenarios.

tasks.title: Implement Linux TPM 2.0 public key extraction
tasks.description: Implement get_public_key() for TPM 2.0 using Esys_ReadPublic() to retrieve the public key from a persistent handle. Convert TPM2B_PUBLIC format to raw 32-byte Ed25519 public key. Validate and cache results.

tasks.title: Implement Linux TPM 2.0 attestation quote generation
tasks.description: Implement generate_attestation() for TPM 2.0 using Esys_Quote() with proper PCR selection. Generate TPM2B_ATTEST structure for inclusion in delegation token field 10. Include signature and qualifying data. Format in AttestationQuote envelope.
acceptance_criteria: Can be validated by remote verifier
Format matches delegation token field 10 spec

tasks.title: Implement Linux TPM 2.0 key management operations
tasks.description: Implement delete_key() using Esys_EvictControl to undefine persistent objects. Implement key_exists() by attempting Esys_ReadPublic. Implement list_keys() by scanning persistent handles. Handle TPM error codes and map to KeyError types.

tasks.title: Implement CryptoEngine pure cryptographic operations API
tasks.description: Create CryptoEngine struct that provides pure cryptographic operations independent of hardware: verify_ed25519, x25519_agree, encrypt_chacha20poly1305, decrypt_chacha20poly1305, encrypt_xchacha20poly1305, decrypt_xchacha20poly1305, hkdf_sha256, argon2id_hash, secure_random, base64url_encode, base64url_decode. This is the main public API for non-hardware-bound crypto.
acceptance_criteria: All crypto functions exposed through CryptoEngine

tasks.title: Implement SessionKeyManager for ephemeral key lifecycle
tasks.description: Create SessionKeyManager for Tier 3 ephemeral session key lifecycle: create_session_keypair() -> (KeyHandle, bytes[32]) generates X25519 keypair in secure memory; derive_session_secret(our_handle: KeyHandle, their_public: bytes[32]) -> bytes[32] performs X25519 agreement; destroy_session(handle: KeyHandle) zeros and frees memory; session_age(handle: KeyHandle) -> Duration; enforce_max_lifetime(max: Duration) enforces 24-hour maximum.
acceptance_criteria: Session keys created in secure memory

tasks.title: Implement secure memory allocation for session keys
tasks.description: Implement secure memory allocation that prevents swapping and ensures zeroization. Use mlock() on Linux to prevent swapping to disk. Use VirtualLock on Windows if needed. Implement custom allocator or wrapper for key material. Track allocated regions for cleanup.

tasks.title: Implement explicit memory zeroization for session keys
tasks.description: Implement guaranteed memory zeroization using platform-specific secure zero functions: memset_s on iOS/macOS, SecureZeroMemory on Windows, explicit_bzero or memset_explicit on Linux. Implement Drop trait for key material to ensure zeroization on scope exit. Prevent compiler optimization from removing zeroization.

tasks.title: Implement session key lifetime enforcement
tasks.description: Enforce maximum 24-hour session key lifetime as per protocol. Track creation timestamp for each session key. Reject operations on expired keys with KeyError::Expired. Implement background cleanup or check on access. Document re-establishment requirements.

tasks.title: Implement HAL factory and platform dispatch
tasks.description: Create HAL factory that instantiates the correct backend based on platform detection. Implement HardwareSecurityModule trait for each backend. Provide singleton or factory pattern for HAL access. Ensure thread-safe access to HAL instance. Handle platform switching for testing.
acceptance_criteria: HAL trait implemented for all backends

tasks.title: Implement thread-safe HAL access and command serialization
tasks.description: Ensure all HAL operations are thread-safe. Implement locking where underlying platforms require serialization (e.g., TPM command serialization). Use RwLock or Mutex appropriately to allow concurrent reads where safe. Prevent deadlocks in multi-threaded scenarios. Test concurrent access patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrent signing operations complete without corruption

tasks.title: Implement Ed25519 EdDSA signing via HAL (MasterKey)
tasks.description: Implement Ed25519 signing for MasterKey (Tier 1) through HAL with biometric gating. Key generation and signing both happen within hardware security boundary. Use COSE algorithm number -8 for EdDSA. Enforce biometric authentication for every signing operation. Return 64-byte signature.

tasks.title: Implement Ed25519 EdDSA signing via HAL (DeviceKey)
tasks.description: Implement Ed25519 signing for DeviceKey (Tier 2) through HAL without biometric re-authentication. Signing happens within TPM/Secure Enclave boundary. Authentication only required at key creation. Return 64-byte signature valid for delegation tokens.

tasks.title: Implement nonce generation for symmetric encryption
tasks.description: Implement cryptographically secure nonce generation for ChaCha20-Poly1305 (12-byte) and XChaCha20-Poly1305 (24-byte). Use secure_random() internally. Prevent nonce reuse through tracking or counter-based generation where appropriate. Document nonce handling requirements.

tasks.title: Implement key capability introspection API
tasks.description: Implement runtime key capability introspection to determine available hardware features. Functions: detect_platform() -> PlatformType, get_capabilities() -> HsmCapabilities, supports_feature(feature: Feature) -> bool. Allow applications to adapt behavior based on available security hardware.

tasks.title: Implement COSE algorithm identifier constants
tasks.description: Define COSE algorithm number constants as specified: EdDSA = -8 for Ed25519 signing, ECDH-ES = -25 for X25519 agreement. Create type-safe enum for algorithm identifiers. Include utility functions for converting between identifiers and algorithm implementations. Document COSE Sign1 header usage.

tasks.title: Implement CBOR serialization utilities
tasks.description: Integrate CBOR library (serde_cbor or similar) for serializing attestation data and delegation token payloads. Implement canonical CBOR encoding per RFC 8949. Create helper functions for common serialization patterns. Ensure deterministic encoding for cryptographic contexts.

tasks.title: Implement COSE Sign1 structure support
tasks.description: Integrate COSE library or implement COSE Sign1 structure construction per RFC 9052. Support protected headers with algorithm identifiers. Create utilities for building and verifying COSE Sign1 structures used in delegation token signing. Handle COSE key objects.

tasks.title: Write unit tests for Base64url encoding/decoding
tasks.description: Comprehensive unit tests for Base64url functions: RFC 4648 test vectors, edge cases (empty input, all-ones, all-zeros), padding rejection, invalid character handling, 32-byte key encoding producing 43 characters. Property-based tests for round-trip encoding.

tasks.title: Write unit tests for HKDF-SHA256
tasks.description: Comprehensive unit tests for HKDF-SHA256 using RFC 5869 test vectors. Test extract phase, expand phase, and combined operation. Test edge cases: empty salt, empty info, maximum output length. Test domain separation produces different keys.

tasks.title: Write unit tests for Argon2id password hashing
tasks.description: Comprehensive unit tests for Argon2id: consistent output for same inputs, different output for different salts, timing verification (longer than reduced parameters), parameter enforcement (64MB, 3 iterations, 4 parallelism), output length verification (32 bytes).

tasks.title: Write unit tests for Ed25519 signature verification
tasks.description: Comprehensive unit tests for Ed25519 verification: RFC 8032 test vectors, valid signatures verify, single bit flips cause rejection, non-canonical encodings rejected, edge cases (small order points, etc.), performance under 1ms. Include wycheproof test vectors if available.

tasks.title: Write unit tests for X25519 key agreement
tasks.description: Comprehensive unit tests for X25519: RFC 7748 test vectors, both parties produce identical shared secret, low-order point rejection, invalid public key rejection, edge cases (all-zeros, all-ones). Test with independent key pairs.

tasks.title: Write unit tests for ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption/decryption
tasks.description: Comprehensive unit tests for ChaCha20-Poly1305: RFC 8439 test vectors, round-trip encryption/decryption, AAD handling, authentication failure on tampered ciphertext, wrong key rejection, wrong nonce rejection, nonce reuse detection.

tasks.title: Write unit tests for XChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption/decryption
tasks.description: Comprehensive unit tests for XChaCha20-Poly1305: round-trip encryption/decryption, 24-byte nonce handling, HChaCha20 subkey derivation, AAD handling, authentication failure detection. Test vectors from libsodium or specifications.

tasks.title: Write integration tests for HAL backend initialization
tasks.description: Integration tests for HAL backend initialization on each platform: detection works, correct backend selected, capabilities accurate, unsupported platforms handled gracefully. Use conditional compilation to run platform-specific tests.

tasks.title: Write integration tests for HAL key lifecycle
tasks.description: Integration tests for complete key lifecycle: generate_key, get_public_key, sign, key_exists, delete_key. Test for both MasterKey and DeviceKey types. Verify keys are non-exportable. Test error cases: duplicate key, deleted key access.

tasks.title: Write integration tests for HAL signing with authentication
tasks.description: Integration tests for signing with biometric/PIN authentication: MasterKey requires auth, DeviceKey does not, auth failure handled, cancellation handled, successful auth produces valid signature. May require mock authentication for CI.

tasks.title: Write integration tests for HAL attestation
tasks.description: Integration tests for attestation generation: attestation produced, format valid, can be validated (where possible), certificate chain present. Test on platforms supporting attestation (TPM 2.0, iOS 14+, Android with StrongBox).

tasks.title: Write integration tests for SessionKeyManager
tasks.description: Integration tests for session key lifecycle: keypair creation, secret derivation, age tracking, lifetime enforcement, proper cleanup. Test X25519 agreement between two session keypairs. Verify zeroization after destroy.

tasks.title: Write memory safety tests for session key zeroization
tasks.description: Memory inspection tests to verify session keys are zeroized within 1 second of session teardown. Use memory scanning or debugging APIs to verify zeroization. Test that keys are never written to persistent storage. May require platform-specific test harnesses.

tasks.title: Write thread safety tests for concurrent operations
tasks.description: Stress tests for thread safety: concurrent signing operations, concurrent key generation, concurrent HAL access, no data corruption, no deadlocks. Use thread sanitizers where available. Test with high contention scenarios.

tasks.title: Write performance benchmarks for Ed25519 verification
tasks.description: Criterion.rs benchmarks for Ed25519 signature verification. Measure latency under various conditions. Verify under 1ms requirement on reference hardware. Track performance regression in CI.

tasks.title: Write performance benchmarks for Argon2id hashing
tasks.description: Criterion.rs benchmarks for Argon2id password hashing with protocol parameters (64MB, 3 iterations, 4 parallelism). Measure execution time. Verify under 3 seconds requirement on reference hardware. Compare with reduced parameters to demonstrate timing difference.
acceptance_criteria: Timing difference vs reduced params shown

tasks.title: Create mock HAL implementation for testing
tasks.description: Create a mock HAL implementation that simulates hardware security module behavior for testing without actual hardware. Support configurable behaviors: success, auth failure, hardware unavailable. Enable testing of higher-level code without platform dependencies.

tasks.title: Create software-only fallback (test-only, never production)
tasks.description: Create a software-only HAL backend for testing purposes ONLY. Clearly marked as test-only with compile-time or runtime guards preventing production use. Uses libsodium in software. Enables CI testing on platforms without hardware security modules. MUST fail in production builds.

tasks.title: Implement audit logging for security operations
tasks.description: Implement audit logging for all security-sensitive operations: key generation, signing (with key type), authentication attempts (success/failure), key deletion, attestation generation. Logs must include timestamp, operation type, key identifier (not material), and result. Ensure logs don't leak sensitive data.

tasks.title: Implement key usage counters and rate limiting
tasks.description: Implement optional key usage counters for security monitoring: track signing operations per key, detect anomalous usage patterns, rate limiting for signing operations to prevent abuse. Configurable thresholds per key type.

tasks.title: Write API documentation for CryptoEngine
tasks.description: Comprehensive rustdoc documentation for CryptoEngine: all public methods documented with examples, security considerations noted, error conditions described, performance characteristics mentioned. Include usage examples for common operations.
